hypothesis: Weighting the DPO reward by Thermo-Calc database-coverage confidence—downweighting candidates whose constituent element combinations lie in low-coverage regions of the TCHEA7 database—will reduce element-token KL drift during preference tuning and produce models that, across architectures (LLaMA, Gemma, OLMo), achieve higher mean Thermo-Calc/CALPHAD-validated viability and greater stability of synthesis-objective satisfaction than standard unweighted DPO.
